understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to use a limited number of moves to maneuver the controllable piece(s) around the grid (and potentially push blocks) so that a selectable piece could reach a goal tile (+), progressing through the level(s) within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, updating its [x,y] pixel position; some moves could fail due to collisions with walls/boundaries or other blocking behavior. If a pushable object (block) was directly adjacent in the move direction, the game could push it along with the selected piece, provided the space beyond was free/not blocked. Walls (#) acted as obstacles, but their exact blocking behavior could vary and needed to be learned by trying moves. When multiple selectable pieces existed, CLICK on a selectable’s exact pixel position switched control to that piece, after which all MOVE actions applied to the newly selected piece.
